title: Foundations of Computer Science
description: Introduction to the theory of computation, including formal languages, automata, computability, and complexity. Covers finite automata, context-free grammars, Turing machines, decidability, and NP-completeness.

title: Introduction to Computer Security
description: Introduction to the principles and practices of computer security as applied to software, host systems, and networks. Covers cryptographic functions, protocols, threats, defenses, incident response, and computer forensics.

title: Introduction to Computer Organization
description: Hardware organization of computer systems. Number systems, machine-level data representation, assembly language programming, memory hierarchy, caching, pipelining, and input/output.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of current interest selected by faculty. Content varies by semester. May be repeated for credit when topics differ.

title: Mobile App Development for Entrepreneurs
description: Build iOS and Android applications using modern frameworks. Project-based course covering UI design, backend integration, app store deployment, and startup pitching. Low weekly workload relative to other ULCS.

title: Introduction to Machine Learning
description: Core machine learning techniques including regression, classification, clustering, neural networks, and deep learning. Covers optimization, model selection, and evaluation on real-world datasets.

title: Conversational Artificial Intelligence
description: Design and implementation of conversational AI systems. Topics include dialogue management, intent detection, slot filling, natural language understanding, and evaluation of chatbot systems.

title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
description: Search algorithms, constraint satisfaction, Bayesian reasoning, probabilistic graphical models, planning, reinforcement learning, and introductory machine learning. Broad survey of AI subfields.

title: Computer Vision
description: Image formation, low-level feature extraction, edge and corner detection, optical flow, object recognition, convolutional neural networks, and 3D reconstruction from images.

title: Database Management Systems
description: Relational model, SQL, query optimization, transaction processing, concurrency control, recovery, and NoSQL systems. Students implement components of a relational database engine.

title: Introduction to Operating Systems
description: Operating system design and implementation: processes, threads, concurrency, synchronization, deadlock, scheduling, memory management, file systems, and security. Several substantial programming projects.

title: Introduction to Operating Systems
description: Multi-tasking, concurrency and synchronization, inter-process communication, deadlock, scheduling, memory and storage management, file systems, and protection. Substantial programming in a concurrent environment.

title: Computer Networks
description: Network architecture and protocols, layered models, TCP/IP, routing algorithms, congestion control, application-layer protocols, network security, and software-defined networking.

title: Web Systems
description: Large-scale web systems: search engines, MapReduce, social networks, content delivery. Covers web crawling, inverted indexes, link analysis, scalable storage, and distributed computing fundamentals.

title: Information Retrieval and Web Search
description: Indexing, retrieval models including BM25 and neural ranking, text classification, question answering, transformer models, and evaluation of search systems. Heavy focus on natural language processing techniques.

title: Special Topics: Computer Networks
description: Advanced topics in computer networking including wireless networks, peer-to-peer systems, network measurement, and emerging network architectures. Content varies by semester.

title: Programming Languages
description: Programming languages as mathematical structures and user interfaces. Covers type systems, operational semantics, functional programming, logic programming, and techniques for reasoning about program behavior.

title: Software Engineering
description: Software development processes, requirements engineering, architecture, design patterns, testing strategies, code review, and project management. Large team project over the semester.

title: User Interface Development
description: Concepts and techniques for designing computer system user interfaces. Task analysis, display and interaction design, usability evaluation, and implementation using an object-oriented application framework.

title: Compiler Construction
description: Lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, type checking, intermediate representations, optimization, and code generation. Students build a complete compiler for a substantial programming language.
